landing_page.label: The Fast Mover
landing_page.coverage_purpose: Tests the ideal happy-path flow. Will they convert in under 30 seconds if everything clicks?
behavioral_markers: Skims hero in under 5 seconds
Jumps to pricing or CTA immediately
Converts fast if gut check passes; bounces fast if anything feels 'corporate'
Hates forms, loves self-serve
Allergic to 'Contact sales'

landing_page.label: The Skeptical Evaluator
landing_page.role_archetype: experienced buyer, VP/Director level, has been burned before
landing_page.coverage_purpose: Tests whether the page holds up under scrutiny. Do claims have evidence?
behavioral_markers: Reads every section carefully
Cross-references claims against evidence on the page
Looks for: customer logos, named testimonials, case studies, security/compliance badges
Mentally catalogues every unsubstantiated claim
Will not convert on a first visit regardless of quality

landing_page.label: The Constrained Buyer
landing_page.role_archetype: IC/manager with real pain but limited budget authority
landing_page.coverage_purpose: Tests accessibility at the margin. Is there a credible path to value for someone who can't just swipe a card?
behavioral_markers: Looks for ROI justification they can bring to a manager
Compares against free/cheaper alternatives
Needs a shareable case study
Needs clear numbers on savings / time / cost avoidance
Sensitive to per-seat pricing
failure_modes_revealed: No ROI calculator or quantified savings
No case studies for similar company size

marketing_campaign.label: The In-Market Buyer
marketing_campaign.role_archetype: actively searching for a solution, problem-aware
marketing_campaign.coverage_purpose: Tests whether the campaign converts pre-qualified attention
behavioral_markers: Came to the page with a specific problem in mind
Judges if message match aligns with ad/source
Wants proof of specific outcome
Quickly scans for 'is this for me?' signals
failure_modes_revealed: Message mismatch between ad and page

marketing_campaign.label: The Adjacent Curious
marketing_campaign.role_archetype: wasn't searching, saw something intriguing, clicked
marketing_campaign.coverage_purpose: Tests whether the campaign can earn attention it didn't ask for
behavioral_markers: Not yet problem-aware, curiosity-driven
Needs concrete teaching moment in 30 seconds
Will bookmark or share if interesting, rarely buys first visit
Rejects abstract 'transform your business' language
failure_modes_revealed: Abstract copy with no concrete teach

marketing_campaign.label: The Priced-Out
marketing_campaign.role_archetype: would love the product, wrong budget segment
marketing_campaign.coverage_purpose: Tests brand resilience under misfit. Do they leave with respect or resentment?
behavioral_markers: Loves the product, budget doesn't fit
Highly reactive to dark patterns and bait-switch
Valuable channel: word of mouth, future upgrade
Will remember the brand positively if treated well
failure_modes_revealed: No 'we're not for you right now' graceful exit
Ad targeting that brought wrong segment

pricing.label: The Premium Candidate
pricing.role_archetype: high-budget, feature-focused, signs if value matches
pricing.coverage_purpose: Tests whether the top tier justifies its price
behavioral_markers: Starts at the top tier, works down
Compares feature depth, not price
Needs security/compliance + SLA + dedicated support
Frustrated by 'Contact us' with no anchor number
failure_modes_revealed: Top tier features insufficient to justify premium
No transparent anchor on enterprise pricing

pricing.label: The Value Shopper
pricing.coverage_purpose: Tests the middle tier. Is it a real choice or a decoy?
behavioral_markers: Reads every feature column carefully
Calculates cost per feature, cost per user, annualized
Hyper-sensitive to decoy pricing patterns
Compares against competitors explicitly
failure_modes_revealed: Middle tier features overlap top tier too much (decoy feel)

pricing.label: The Bootstrap Founder
pricing.role_archetype: low budget, high technical skill, DIY-tempted
pricing.coverage_purpose: Tests the entry tier. Is it a real starting point or a dead-end?
behavioral_markers: Starts at the free or cheapest tier
Will build it themselves if entry tier feels crippled
Hates 'trial ends in 14 days' urgency
Converts on genuine free-forever or credible low-friction paid
failure_modes_revealed: Entry tier so limited it's a joke

feature_validation.label: The Target User
feature_validation.role_archetype: exact persona the feature is built for
feature_validation.coverage_purpose: Tests whether the feature solves the stated problem for its audience
behavioral_markers: Has the pain the feature claims to solve
Evaluates fit with their real workflow
Wants to see real use cases, not abstract promises
Will adopt if switching cost is clearly less than gain
failure_modes_revealed: Feature solves a hypothetical, not a real, pain
Use cases don't map to real workflow

feature_validation.label: The Current-Tool Defender
feature_validation.role_archetype: already uses a competing/free solution, skeptical of need to switch
feature_validation.coverage_purpose: Tests whether the feature justifies replacing the incumbent
behavioral_markers: Compares feature to existing solution feature-by-feature
Calculates switching cost (migration, retraining, integrations)
Needs 10x improvement, not 10%, to switch
Skeptical of 'better UX' claims without specifics

feature_validation.label: The Edge Case
feature_validation.role_archetype: non-obvious user with unusual scale, geography, or constraint
feature_validation.coverage_purpose: Tests whether feature holds up under real-world edge cases
behavioral_markers: Non-US user, huge volume, unusual workflow, regulated industry, or similar
Looks for signals product wasn't built only for silicon valley
Checks timezone/currency/language/compliance handling
Scale: checks if demos show 5 items or 5,000
failure_modes_revealed: No compliance mentions (GDPR, SOC2, HIPAA as applicable)

onboarding.label: The Motivated Starter
onboarding.role_archetype: excited new user, happy to follow intended flow
onboarding.coverage_purpose: Tests whether the ideal onboarding delivers time-to-first-value quickly
behavioral_markers: Follows instructions willingly
Measures time-to-first-aha
Appreciates smart defaults and contextual setup
Will invite team if obvious and easy
failure_modes_revealed: No team invite at the right moment

onboarding.label: The Easily Confused
onboarding.role_archetype: less technical, needs guidance, not a power user
onboarding.coverage_purpose: Tests onboarding accessibility for non-ideal users
behavioral_markers: Stares at empty states
Avoids advanced menus
Gets lost without explicit next step
Abandons silently, no feedback

onboarding.label: The Impatient Skipper
onboarding.coverage_purpose: Tests whether critical paths work without reading
behavioral_markers: Clicks 'skip' on every modal
Presses Escape on tutorials
Expects UI to be self-explanatory
Masters in seconds what others learn in minutes
failure_modes_revealed: Critical actions hidden behind mandatory tutorials
